papers.title: Noncanonical role of Arabidopsis COP1/SPA complex in repressing BIN2-mediated PIF3 phosphorylation and degradation in darkness

edges.mechanism: Pfr-phyB sequesters PIF1 from target promoters and triggers phosphorylation/proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB inhibits the binding of PIF1 and PIF3 to their target promoters in a light-dependent manner; phyB also reduces PIF1 protein levels in red light

edges.mechanism: Pfr-phyB binds PIF3 via APB motif, triggers phosphorylation and 26S proteasome degradation, and sequesters PIF3 from target promoters
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF3 exhibited light-induced phosphorylation and degradation

edges.mechanism: Pfr-phyB triggers PIF4 degradation in red light; warm temperatures release this inhibition
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB levels are increased by 1.6 to 1.8-fold in pif4 relative to wild-type levels

edges.mechanism: Pfr-phyB induces PIF5 phosphorylation and proteasomal degradation in red light
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF4 and PIF5 are direct substrates of phytochrome-mediated phosphorylation

edges.mechanism: Pfr-phyB induces dephosphorylation/sequestration of PIF7 in light; also triggers nuclear speckle migration
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF7 rapidly (within 2 min) forms speckles when first exposed to a brief, saturating red light

edges.mechanism: PIFs negatively feed back on phyB protein levels, promoting phyB degradation in prolonged red light
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB levels are increased by 1.6-fold in pif3 relative to wild-type levels

edges.mechanism: PIF4 binds phyB Pfr via APB motif and accelerates phyB turnover
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB levels are increased 1.6 to 1.8-fold in pif4

edges.mechanism: PIF7 contributes to phyB degradation under prolonged red light
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB levels are increased 1.11-fold in pif7

edges.mechanism: PIF1 is a basic helix-loop-helix TF promoting skotomorphogenesis; loss of PIF1 confers shorter dark-grown hypocotyls
edges.evidence_sentence: PIFs are basic helix-loop-helix transcription factors that regulate thousands of genes to promote skotomorphogenesis

edges.mechanism: PIF3 promotes hypocotyl elongation in dark and under low Pfr levels
edges.evidence_sentence: pif3 mutants are hypersensitive to all Rc fluence rates tested

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly activates auxin biosynthesis (YUC8) and cell wall modification (XTR7) genes that promote hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: pif4 pif5 mutants exhibited reduced hypocotyl elongation compared with the WT in the absence of UV-B

edges.mechanism: PIF5 acts redundantly with PIF4 to activate cell elongation genes
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF4 and PIF5 are known negative regulators of photomorphogenesis that mediate hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: PIF7 promotes hypocotyl elongation, particularly in shade and warm temperatures
edges.evidence_sentence: pif7 mutant seedlings displayed shorter hypocotyls than wild-type seedlings when grown under Rc

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly binds G-box in YUC8 promoter to activate auxin biosynthesis
edges.evidence_sentence: YUC8 expression reduced in pif4 pif5 double mutants under white light

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly binds IAA19 promoter G-box
edges.evidence_sentence: ChIP shows reduced PIF4/PIF5 association with IAA19 promoter under UV-B

edges.mechanism: PIF4 directly activates IAA29 transcription via G-box binding
edges.evidence_sentence: direct targets of PIF4 and/or PIF5; IAA29 expression dependent on PIF4/PIF5

edges.mechanism: PIF4 binds G-box in XTR7 (xyloglucan endotransglucosylase) promoter to activate cell wall remodeling
edges.evidence_sentence: association of PIF4-HA with XTR7 promoter elements was reduced in response to UV-B

edges.mechanism: PIF5 directly binds XTR7 promoter G-box
edges.evidence_sentence: G-boxes approximately 1.9 kb upstream of XTR7 start ATG directly targeted by PIF5

edges.mechanism: Cell wall xyloglucan endotransglucosylase activity supports cell wall loosening for elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: xyloglucan endotransglycosylase-related gene family such as XTR7 linked to hypocotyl elongation

edges.mechanism: Red light photoconverts inactive Pr to active Pfr form of phyB
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB induces light responses across a wide range of light conditions

edges.mechanism: Far-red light photoactivates phyA
edges.evidence_sentence: phyA mediates far-red light responses

edges.mechanism: Blue light photoreduces FAD chromophore of CRY1 to active state
edges.evidence_sentence: Upon absorption of photons, cryptochromes are photoexcited involving electron transfer

edges.mechanism: Blue light photoactivates CRY2 (and triggers CRY2 turnover at high fluence)
edges.evidence_sentence: cryptochromes are photoexcited involving electron transfer

edges.mechanism: UV-B causes UVR8 dimer to monomerize and activates UV-B signaling
edges.evidence_sentence: In planta, UV-B stimulates rapid nuclear accumulation of the UVR8 protein

edges.mechanism: CRY1 mediates blue-light inhibition of hypocotyl elongation, primarily via inactivating COP1-SPA and stabilizing HY5
edges.evidence_sentence: Arabidopsis cry1 (hy4) mutants exhibit elongated hypocotyl under blue light

edges.mechanism: CRY2 mediates blue-light hypocotyl inhibition at low fluence rates and photoperiod control of flowering
edges.evidence_sentence: cry2 mutation shows evident effects under medium-low fluence blue light

edges.mechanism: UVR8 perceives UV-B and triggers UV-B-induced hypocotyl growth inhibition via COP1 and HY5
edges.evidence_sentence: hypocotyl growth of the uvr8 mutant seedlings was not inhibited by UV-B

edges.mechanism: phyB primarily inhibits hypocotyl elongation in red and white light by sequestering and degrading PIFs
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB primary photoreceptor for inhibition of hypocotyl elongation in red light

edges.mechanism: phyA mediates far-red light inhibition of hypocotyl elongation; phyA mutants have long hypocotyls under FR
edges.evidence_sentence: phyA phyB double mutants produce longer hypocotyls than wild type

edges.mechanism: UV-B-activated UVR8 monomer directly binds COP1 and reorients COP1 substrate specificity away from HY5
edges.evidence_sentence: wild type but not the mutant UVR8 and COP1 proteins directly interact in a UV-B-dependent rapid manner in planta

edges.mechanism: Blue-light-activated CRY1 binds SPA and disrupts COP1-SPA complex, blocking ubiquitination of HY5
edges.evidence_sentence: light-dependent suppression of protein degradation via CRY-SPA1/COP1 pathway

edges.mechanism: CRY2 binds SPA-COP1 complex to inhibit substrate ubiquitination
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1 interacts directly or indirectly with cryptochromes

edges.mechanism: Active phyB drives COP1 nuclear export, reducing COP1-mediated HY5 degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: phyB and other photoreceptors antagonize COP1 in the nucleus

edges.mechanism: Active phyA inhibits COP1-mediated HY5/LAF1/HFR1 degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: FAR1, FHY1 transmit phyA signals to inactivate COP1

edges.mechanism: COP1-SPA E3 ubiquitin ligase polyubiquitinates HY5, targeting it for 26S proteasomal degradation in dark
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1 targets different photomorphogenesis-promoting transcription factors for degradation in the dark, among them HY5

edges.mechanism: COP1 ubiquitinates HYH for proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1-SPA complex interacts with and promotes ubiquitination and degradation of HY5/HYH

edges.mechanism: COP1 ubiquitinates LAF1 for proteasomal degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1 targets photomorphogenesis-promoting transcription factors LAF1 for ubiquitination

edges.mechanism: COP1 ubiquitinates HFR1 (LONG HYPOCOTYL IN FAR RED) for degradation
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1 targets HFR1 for degradation

edges.mechanism: SPA1 directly phosphorylates HY5 at Ser-36, stabilizing HY5 but reducing its DNA-binding activity
edges.evidence_sentence: SPA1 directly phosphorylates HY5 in vitro in a concentration and time-dependent manner

edges.mechanism: SPA1 forms heteromeric complex with COP1 enhancing E3 ligase activity toward HY5
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1-SPA complex interacts with and promotes ubiquitination and degradation of HY5

edges.mechanism: HY5 is a master bZIP transcription factor that activates hundreds of light-responsive genes promoting photomorphogenesis (cotyledon expansion, anthocyanin) while inhibiting hypocotyl elongation
edges.evidence_sentence: HY5 is a key positive regulator that promotes photomorphogenesis; hy5 mutant showed relatively longer hypocotyl

edges.mechanism: HYH (HY5 HOMOLOG) acts redundantly with HY5 to inhibit hypocotyl growth in light
edges.evidence_sentence: HY5 and its homologue HYH are key effectors of the UVR8 pathway

edges.mechanism: DET1 stabilizes PIF1 protein in dark; det1 reduces PIF1 abundance
edges.evidence_sentence: DET1 positively regulates all four PIFs at the protein level via posttranslational regulation

edges.mechanism: DET1 positively regulates PIF3 transcription and protein stability
edges.evidence_sentence: DET1 was shown to positively regulate PIF3 at the transcriptional level

edges.mechanism: DET1 stabilizes PIF4 protein in dark
edges.evidence_sentence: all four PIF-Myc proteins decayed significantly more rapidly in det1-1 than in wild-type background

edges.mechanism: DET1 stabilizes PIF5 protein in dark
edges.evidence_sentence: all four PIF-Myc proteins decayed significantly more rapidly in det1-1

edges.mechanism: DET1 represses photomorphogenesis in dark by stabilizing PIFs; det1 loss yields constitutive photomorphogenesis (short hypocotyl in dark)
edges.evidence_sentence: Arabidopsis seedlings undergo photomorphogenic development even in darkness when DET1 function is disrupted

edges.mechanism: COP1 represses photomorphogenesis in dark by degrading HY5/HYH/LAF1/HFR1; cop1 loss → short hypocotyl in dark (constitutive photomorphogenesis)
edges.evidence_sentence: cop1-4 mutants show shorter hypocotyls and fully opened cotyledons in darkness

edges.mechanism: SPA1-4 promote COP1-mediated degradation of HY5; spaQ mutants are constitutively photomorphogenic (short hypocotyl in dark)
edges.evidence_sentence: spaQ mutant shows constitutive photomorphogenesis

edges.mechanism: BIN2 GSK3 kinase phosphorylates PIF3 at T220/S224 and S283/S287 motifs, triggering 26S proteasome degradation in skotomorphogenesis
edges.evidence_sentence: BIN2 kinase induces PIF3 degradation via 26S proteasome during skotomorphogenesis

edges.mechanism: COP1 directly binds BIN2 N-terminus and inhibits BIN2 kinase activity toward PIF3 in dark
edges.evidence_sentence: COP1 directly interacts with BIN2 in vitro and in vivo; presence of COP1/SPA reduces BIN2-PIF3 interaction

edges.mechanism: SPA1 competes with BIN2 for PIF3 binding, reducing BIN2-mediated PIF3 phosphorylation
edges.evidence_sentence: SPA1 presence reduces BIN2-PIF3 interaction through competing for same PIF3 regions

edges.mechanism: UVR8 destabilizes PIF4 protein under UV-B (mRNA unchanged)
edges.evidence_sentence: UV-B decreased their levels in the WT but not in uvr8 mutant backgrounds

edges.mechanism: UVR8 destabilizes PIF5 protein under UV-B
edges.evidence_sentence: PIF4-HA and PIF5-HA protein levels decrease under UV-B in UVR8-dependent manner

edges.mechanism: UV-B-activated UVR8 stabilizes HY5 protein and promotes HY5 transcription via COP1 inactivation
edges.evidence_sentence: UVR8 associates constitutively with chromatin regions of several UV-B-activated genes, including the HY5 genomic locus

perturbations.evidence_sentence: phyB phyA double mutants produce longer hypocotyls than wild type in Rc
phyB-9 mutant shows constitutively long hypocotyls in light
phyA mutant has long hypocotyls under far-red
phyA-211 phyB-9 produces longer hypocotyls than wild type in Rc
PHYBG111D-OX has short hypocotyls with open cotyledons
Arabidopsis cry1 (hy4) mutants exhibit elongated hypocotyl under blue light
cry2 mutation shows evident effects under medium-low fluence blue light
cry1cry2 double mutants have stronger long-hypocotyl phenotypes than either single mutant
overexpression of Arabidopsis CRY1 resulted in enhanced light responses
transgenic seedlings overexpressing CRY2 showed enhanced light responses
hypocotyl growth of the uvr8 mutant seedlings was not inhibited by UV-B
marked UV-B photomorphogenic hypersensitivity in all assays employed, including hypocotyl growth inhibition
cop1-4 mutants show shorter hypocotyls and fully opened cotyledons in darkness
cop1 mutants are deficient in UV-B photomorphogenic responses
hy5 displayed longer hypocotyls even in the absence of UV-B
hy5 hyh double mutant still showed obvious inhibition of hypocotyl growth under UV-B
hy5 hyh double mutant has longer hypocotyls
Arabidopsis seedlings undergo photomorphogenic development even in darkness when DET1 function is disrupted
pif1-1 det1-1 double mutant exhibited more exaggerated photomorphogenic phenotypes
pif3 mutants are hypersensitive to all Rc fluence rates tested
pif4 had slightly greater sensitivity to Rc
pif4 pif5 mutants exhibited reduced hypocotyl elongation
pif7 mutant seedlings displayed shorter hypocotyls than wild-type seedlings when grown under Rc
quadruple pifq mutant displays development in total darkness that strongly phenocopies normal light-grown wild-type seedlings (short hypocotyls, open cotyledons)
PIF3-OX shows longer hypocotyls in light
PIF4-OX promotes hypocotyl elongation
Constitutively active bin2-1 mutant dramatically decreases PIF3 abundance; longer hypocotyl
spaQ mutant shows constitutive photomorphogenesis; phosphorylation of HY5 abolished
